evals.prompt: Critique this idea: I want to build an internal tool at Red Hat that uses AI agents to automatically generate usability test reports from Jira acceptance criteria. The agents would take a STRAT ticket, extract the ACs, run Playwright walkthroughs against a running prototype, score 7 usability dimensions, and produce an HTML report. This would replace manual usability evaluations that currently take researchers 2-3 hours per prototype.
evals.expected_output: 3-5 specific critiques with research backing, a clear verdict, and de-risk suggestions
assertions: Output contains between 3 and 5 numbered critiques
Each critique has a 'What if' scenario describing what could go wrong
Each critique has a 'De-risk it' suggestion with a concrete action
Output contains a verdict section with one of: 'strong enough to commit', 'needs one fix', or 'rethink this part'
At least one critique references real-world data, research, or precedent from web search
No HTML files are generated (output is conversational only)
Output starts with a 2-sentence summary of the idea being critiqued

evals.prompt: Stress test this: We're thinking about switching our entire frontend from React to Svelte because one engineer tried it on a side project and liked it. The main app is 200k lines of React code with 50+ components. We'd do it incrementally over 6 months.
evals.expected_output: Sharp critiques about migration risk, team readiness, and opportunity cost with research grounding
assertions: Output contains between 3 and 5 numbered critiques
At least one critique addresses the scale of migration (200k lines)
At least one critique questions the evidence basis (one engineer's side project)
No dashes are used as clause separators or dramatic pauses
Tone is curious and questioning, not hostile or dismissive

evals.prompt: Poke holes in this: I want to create a Slack bot that summarizes every channel at the end of the day and posts a digest. This would save our 50-person team from reading hundreds of messages across 20 channels.
evals.expected_output: Critiques covering privacy, signal-to-noise, adoption risk, and simpler alternatives
assertions: Output contains between 3 and 5 numbered critiques
At least one critique addresses privacy or sensitivity of auto-summarizing conversations
Each critique includes both a 'What if' and 'De-risk it' section
No HTML files or artifacts are generated
